faqs.question: What is futures trading?

blocks.value: Futures trading allows users to buy or sell contracts that represent an underlying cryptocurrency at a predetermined price. It enables traders to speculate on price movements without owning the actual asset.

faqs.question: How does futures trading work?

value: Traders open long positions if they expect prices to rise
Traders open short positions if they expect prices to fall
Profit and loss are determined by the difference between the entry price and exit price of the contract

faqs.question: What is leverage in futures trading?

blocks.value: Leverage allows you to control a larger position using a smaller amount of capital.
For example, 10× leverage allows you to open a position worth 10 times your margin.
Higher leverage increases both potential profits and potential losses.

faqs.question: What is margin?

blocks.value: Margin is the collateral required to open and maintain a futures position.

value: Initial Margin: Capital required to open a position
Maintenance Margin: Minimum balance required to keep the position open

blocks.value: If margin falls below maintenance requirements, liquidation may occur.

faqs.question: What is liquidation?

blocks.value: Liquidation occurs when your account equity falls below the maintenance margin.
The system will automatically close your position to prevent further losses.

faqs.question: Can I lose more than my margin?

blocks.value: Yes. Futures trading involves the risk of losing more than your initial margin, especially when using high leverage or cross margin mode.
Users should monitor positions closely and manage risk carefully.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Cross Margin and Isolated Margin?

value: Cross Margin: All available margin is shared across positions, which may reduce liquidation risk but can result in larger losses
Isolated Margin: Margin is limited to a single position, restricting maximum loss to the allocated amount

blocks.value: Margin mode availability depends on platform rules.

faqs.question: Can I switch margin mode after opening a position?

blocks.value: Margin mode switching may be restricted once a position is open.
Please check platform rules before opening a position.

faqs.question: Can I add margin to an existing position?

blocks.value: Yes. Adding margin can help reduce liquidation risk, subject to platform limits and account balance availability.

faqs.question: What order types are supported in futures trading?

blocks.value: Common order types include:

value: Market Orders – Execute immediately at market price
Limit Orders – Execute at a specified price
Stop / Stop-Limit Orders – Trigger when a preset price is reached

blocks.value: Availability may vary by contract.

faqs.question: What fees apply to futures trading?

blocks.value: Futures trading may include:
All fees are displayed before order confirmation.

faqs.question: What is a funding rate?

blocks.value: Funding rates are periodic payments exchanged between long and short position holders in perpetual contracts.
They help keep futures prices aligned with spot prices.

faqs.question: How often are funding fees charged?

blocks.value: Funding fees are charged at scheduled intervals (e.g., every 8 hours, 4pm, 12am, 8am GMT +8).
The rate may be positive or negative depending on market conditions.

faqs.question: What happens if a position is liquidated?

blocks.value: When liquidation occurs:

value: Positions may be partially or fully closed
Margin is used to cover losses

blocks.value: Remaining funds, if any, are returned to your account.

faqs.question: What is Auto-Deleveraging (ADL)?

blocks.value: Auto-Deleveraging (ADL) is a risk management mechanism that may reduce opposing positions when the system cannot cover losses through liquidation alone.
ADL is triggered during extreme market conditions.

faqs.question: What happens during extreme market volatility?

blocks.value: During high volatility:

value: ADL or forced position reductions may be triggered
Trading or order placement may be temporarily restricted

blocks.value: These measures help maintain platform stability.

faqs.question: Why does my PnL differ from expected results?

blocks.value: PnL differences may occur due to:
Actual results are based on executed trades, not estimated values.

faqs.question: Which assets support futures trading?

blocks.value: Only selected trading pairs support futures contracts.
Available contracts, leverage limits, and margin requirements are shown on the Futures Trading page.

faqs.question: Are there leverage or position size limits?

blocks.value: Yes. Leverage and position size limits depend on:
Limits are displayed on the trading interface.

faqs.question: How do I monitor my futures positions?

blocks.value: You can view:
All information is available in the Futures Dashboard.

faqs.question: Is futures trading available to all users?

blocks.value: Futures trading availability may depend on:
Certain users may be restricted from futures trading.

faqs.question: Is futures trading suitable for beginners?

blocks.value: Futures trading is high-risk and recommended for experienced traders.
Beginners should start with low leverage and fully understand margin and liquidation mechanics.

faqs.question: How do I get help with futures trading issues?

blocks.value: Please contact customer support and provide:
This helps resolve issues efficiently.
